name: Fuentidueña MTB Route
name_original: San Miguel de Bernuy, embalse de las Vencias y Castillo de Fuentidueña — circular desde Adrados
description: This moderate 26.8-mile mountain biking route from Adrados offers reservoir views and a glimpse of Castillo de Fuentidueña.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Adrados offers access to diverse landscapes within the Segovia province, characterized by river valleys and open plains. The region features varied terrain, including areas carved by the Pirón, Cega, and Duratón rivers, which contribute to a network of natural trails. While Adrados itself is a rural setting, its surroundings provide opportunities for mountain biking across different elevations and surfaces. The area's proximity to natural parks like Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park suggests a landscape with impressive canyons and rugged sections. Best mountain bike trails around Adrados The most popular mountain bike trail is Fuentidueña MTB Route, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied landscapes typical of the Segovia countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Laguna Lucía – Laguna del Carrizal loop from Olombrada, a moderate 26.3 miles (42.3 km) path. This route offers views of the local lagoons and open plains. Local mountain bikers also love the Olombrada loop from Frumales, a 21.1 miles (34.0 km) trail leading through rural agricultural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Adrados is defined by open plains, river valleys, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to moderate. The routes in Adrados are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 14 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Adrados's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Adrados?
A: The mountain bike trails around Adrados offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll encounter open plains, river valleys carved by the Pirón, Cega, and Duratón rivers, and rural agricultural areas. While Adrados itself is rural, its proximity to natural parks like the Sierra de Guadarrama means you can find more rugged sections, including granite massifs and extensive forests of pines and oaks, especially if you venture further into the national park areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in the Adrados region?
A: The region around Adrados is generally favorable for mountain biking from late April to mid-November. During this period, you can expect mild summers, allowing for pleasant rides through lush landscapes. Winter mountain biking might be possible, but conditions can be more challenging, especially at higher elevations within the Sierra de Guadarrama where snow can occur.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails near Adrados?
A: Yes, the area offers options suitable for families. While many routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available. The varied terrain, including open plains and rural paths, provides a good environment for less experienced riders or families looking for a more relaxed outing. Always check the specific trail details for elevation and technical difficulty to ensure it matches your family's skill level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the Adrados region, especially on rural paths and in open natural areas. However, if you plan to ride within protected areas like the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park or Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, it's advisable to check specific regulations regarding dogs, as some sections might have restrictions, particularly during wildlife breeding seasons. Always keep your dog under control and clean up after them.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Adrados are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Lucía – Laguna del Carrizal loop from Olombrada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olombrada loop from Frumales&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that take you through varied landscapes.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Adrados vary in length and duration. You can find routes ranging from approximately 21 miles (34 km) to over 26 miles (43 km). For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092095011/fuentiduena-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentidueña MTB Route&lt;/a&gt; is about 26.8 miles (43.1 km) and typically takes around 3 hours 43 minutes to complete, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31478897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Grande loop from Lastras de Cuéllar&lt;/a&gt; is 20.3 miles (32.7 km).

Q: What is the difficulty level of mountain bike trails near Adrados?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails around Adrados are rated as moderate, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility. There is also at least one easy trail available, making the area suitable for various skill levels. While there are no routes classified as difficult directly in Adrados, more challenging terrain with significant elevation gains can be found further afield in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints along the trails?
A: While specific highlights are not detailed for every route, the region is rich in natural beauty. The area is characterized by river valleys and open plains, with opportunities to see lagoons like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Lucía – Laguna del Carrizal loop&lt;/a&gt;. Venturing into nearby natural parks like Hoces del Río Duratón or Sierra de Guadarrama offers impressive canyons, dramatic gorges, and panoramic vistas from higher elevations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Adrados?
A: The mountain biking experience in Adrados is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, which includes open plains, river valleys, and rural landscapes, providing a diverse and engaging ride. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to moderate, making it enjoyable for a wide range of riders.

Q: Are there facilities like parking, cafes, or accommodation available for cyclists in the Adrados area?
A: Adrados and its surrounding towns embody the rural charm of Segovia province, offering a tranquil setting. While specific parking details for each trail are not always listed, you can generally find parking in the villages where routes begin. The area also provides rural accommodations and local establishments, including cafes and pubs, which cater to visitors and cyclists looking to refuel or stay overnight.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or other water bodies?
A: Yes, some routes in the area incorporate water features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Vencias Reservoir – Fuentidueña loop from Fuentesaúco de Fuentidueña&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Las Vencias Reservoir, providing a scenic element to your ride. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Lucía – Laguna del Carrizal loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by local lagoons.
tips: Beautiful views of the Vencias reservoir rolling along a path parallel to it. A real luxury for mountain bike enthusiasts.
Beautiful views of the Vencias reservoir next to the Chiringuito El rincón de Pibe, an ideal place to take a break before continuing with the route.
Nice views of the wall of the Fuentidueña Castle rolling from the dirt road that leaves the Puerta de Alfonso VIII.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 43.1 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 43 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. It involves a total elevation gain of about 430 meters, making it suitable for riders with some experience looking for a good challenge.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends near Adrados. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking in or around the village of Adrados, which serves as a convenient trailhead for this loop.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include a beautiful View of Embalse de las Vencias, the scenic Las Vencias Reservoir itself, and a View of Castillo de Fuentidueña.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or in areas with wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking route. It is open to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to ride this trail are typically spring and autumn when the weather is milder. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though the region generally has a dry climate.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll ride along parts of the Camino al Embalse de las Vencias, Camino de Los Valles, Camino de Fuentepiñel, Camino Canuelo, Calle Callerío, and Camino de Fuentesaúco.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: As a mountain biking route, you can expect a mix of unpaved tracks, gravel paths, and potentially some singletrack sections. The terrain will vary, offering a diverse riding experience suitable for a moderate difficulty level.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Adrados and San Miguel de Bernuy. You may find small local cafes, bars, or rural accommodation options in these areas, particularly in Adrados where the route starts.

faqs.question: Is the route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 43.1 km distance and moderate difficulty rating with over 400 meters of elevation gain, this route is generally more suited for experienced mountain bikers. It might be too challenging for younger children or families looking for a very easy ride.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse de las Vencias
